item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I giggled like a schoolboy throughout the whole thing. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: that's some really bad japanese

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you! I'm glad you enjoyed it, and I appreciate the kind words.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh my god. Reading this, I had a level of apprehension throughout the first half, thinking it was going to be the writer living out a fantasy through an SCP. While not inherently a bad thing, it was seeming very on the nose, and I was cringing slightly.

Once it got going however, I realised it was satire, and I thoroughly enjoyed it. You toed the line between Damien being a hardcore nerd and a full-blown weeaboo really well, the fact that he's not taking the situation completely seriously (fucking DEATH KNIFE… oh my god, I just realised it's a play on DEATH NOTE), and thinking it's an anime. Making Damen actually seem like a good guy and not just a dick was definitely the right call. And then Hanzo being ready to cut a bitch for calling them cartoons! Fucking hilarious. This is the best example of a humourous SCP I've ever seen, hands down.

My head canon is Damien slowly works his way up through the ranks of the Foundation, eventually ending up on the Overseer council.

+1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: cool

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm torn.

Seeing the direction it was going in, I was hoping that the SCP was either a reality bender or just messing with the PoI. Morrocan knife with a samurai ghost in it? That just screamed deception, and I was looking forward to seeing what would happen.

But then there was no twist, just a convoluted punch line.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"PoI-3445: Like, dangerous testing? Don’t I have to sign something for that?

"Dr. Trenton: (Laughs) Not here."

lmfao

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love that you played it straight. I was waiting for the twist, that Daniel's an unknown reality bender or the knife is a story come to life or what have you, and I'm so glad it didn't come. There's something very funny about the whole world bending over backwards just to make one dude's fantasies come true. And I think we all want to be told we're not useless by an ancient samurai ghost, just a little bit. Happy +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I admit, I groaned out loud when I realized this was a Mall Ninja SCP, but was won over by the end, not in small part due to the sheer commitment to the concept, complete with a mall spawning endless shadow demons for the subject to ninja in.

A surprisingly moving speech from the samurai ghost as well.

Bonus points for: "This is a judgement free place." and "Do you get what I'm saying?" "No."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's just like one of my Japanese animes!!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have a huge soft spot for funny scps in general, so I was chuckling and smiling all the way through this, but when I got to the interview with the ghost samurai and ordered them to use the term "anime" I literally audibly started to laugh at the mental image

This went in a completely different direction than I expected; made me laugh; and made me smile as well. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really enjoyed this! I expected this to go a certain way. In the end, POI-3445 would be revealed to be a reality bender, manifesting SCP-5175-1 and the effects of SCP-5175 as an externalized representation of how he WANTED to be seen, based on the personality you reveal through the interviews. But I think the way you took it was MUCH better. Enthusiastic +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't do it. -what Dr. King would say

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So if I see a "Steamtown Cutlery Pagoda" in front of the Aquarium, I'll know what it's about…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have a young adult son who would have just plotzed to have been chosen for the DEATH BLADE. That may have enhanced my enjoyment of this article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was wondering how a rewrite was going to work when you requested it, but I never pictured this. This is amazing. Definitely a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is greatly improved from the draft version that I read. I think portraying Damien as more sympathetic is a vastly better narrative take than the original version, and I think the article is more internally consistent for it.

Still, as I mentioned, there's a knife's edge of making an annoying but still somewhat sympathetic character, and I think this just leans too far into the annoying for my own taste. I completely understand that this is meant to be a satire of a certain mindset, but I think it's possible to have a satire of something annoying that is so close to reality that it bleeds over into making the writing itself difficult to read. It's not quite in that territory since the writing is, all in all, quite solid, but something about the character is still a bit too much for my own taste.

item.title: Rieview - Site Crit
item.content:encoded:
I think is absolutely exemplary of mainlist articles that are funny. Not everything has to be a -J!

The heart of this article lies in the well written and appropriately absurd dialogue, and as Grigori points out above, there are absolute gems in here. It's a great blend of Katana from Suicide Squad and some more self aware taking the piss.

That being said - it's not perfect. As I mentioned above, this is is carried by the strength of the dialogue and interviews. I feel that it drags a bit towards the end. The final two logs, especially, feel like they're building up to something, but ultimately… Nothing comes of it? Nothing really… happens in this article. But that's ok here, because it is otherwise a delight.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What a hilariously entertaining story. I'm upset about the raccoon, but in the best way possible!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Look, I am weird with comedy. I often don't like many comedy movies… so this is the disclaimer in case you wanna ignore my opinion entirely:

I literally LoL'd 15 times reading through this in draft form. I can't even spend the 15 minutes it would take to copy paste all the great lines in this but my favorite:

PoI-3445 brandishes an ordinary kitchen knife from his sweater pocket, and stabs it into the table.

Dr. Trenton: (Distressed) Where the hell did you get that? I thought they frisked you.

PoI-3445: Backup knife.

BACKUP KNIFE

We have all known a Damien (maybe even been a Damien) and if your response to this is "ugh, cliche" you missed the joke.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: DAMIAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAN. nice article +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to everyone who looked over this. Etoile, Jackal, Weizhong, Riemann, Grigori, Pedagon, and Plague.

This is a rewrite of an article by OpMateo8080 from a few weeks ago. I've obtained the original authors permission here. I'll localize if this survives.
